oracle_campaign: Brazil MST — narrative reframe around organic food (2010s–present)
Overall Confidence Explanation: Ten of 15 variables are at High confidence; three are at Medium with clearly stated structural reasons; two are Mixed/Unresolved per the open campaign protocol. Source diversity spans peer-reviewed academic (Sources 2, 3), independent long-form investigative journalism (Source 9), specialist features (Sources 8, 11), NGO and international coalition reporting (Sources 4, 5, 10), movement-adjacent documentation (Sources 1, 6), and independent Brazilian press (Source 7). All 11 locked sources contribute variable-level coding evidence. The three errors identified in the prior coding are fully corrected: the fabricated "5 million families" COVID figure is replaced with the sourced 6,000–7,000 tonnes confirmed across four sources; the understated "400,000+" membership figure is replaced with the sourced characterisation of up to 2 million people; claims attributed to Source 9 are now fully verified from the live-fetched article text.

Ten of fourteen substantive variables sit at High confidence, four at Medium, and no variables are coded Insufficient. Source diversity is strong across all four required categories: campaign primary documentation (Sources 1, 6, 9, 10, 11), independent academic peer-reviewed research (Sources 2, 3), independent technical expert body (Source 8), and independent database/trade press (Sources 5, 7). The campaign resolved cleanly within the window — ZDHC established, 80+ brands committed, Source 11 provides a defined closing statement — so no Mixed/Unresolved coding was required. The four Medium-confidence codings each reflect genuine evidential limitations (no opposition-side source, no coalition map, no cost quantification, no pre-campaign polling) rather than ambiguity about the underlying variable.
